item.title: Boycotts Work! Target Feeling the Pain
item.description:
People always say What can I do, I'm just one person. My group of women have been boycotting Target for months after they removed their DEI policies. We are all the primary purchaser in the house, everything from food to where we go on vacation is influenced by us. You and you friends have the same power and control.
Also check out this awesome artwork @emeraldpixiedust7 created https://imgur.com/gallery/mQX6bds I'm thinking of getting shirts made, if anyone wants in let me know https://fortune.com/2025/04/01/target-dei-demise-boycott-foot-traffic-down-eighth-consecutive-week/
item.dc:creator: tallyhoho

item.title: Money

item.title: I know that the government favors the 1%, but seeking the death penalty for Luigi surprised me a bit.
item.description: Video: https://youtu.be/hrzaQe5fv9c?si=PS0HPUvMISTcjEri . I saw this in my YouTube feed, and did a heavy eye roll. For those of you that don’t know me, I did a ten year project examining Texas death row details. I even made a post about the last words of people who were executed by the state. Link: https://imgur.com/gallery/gj5OwZQ . It never ceases to amaze me at how blatantly the government favors the 1%. Having gone through a list of over 500 death row convictions myself, I’m telling you that regardless of whether you are for/against the death penalty, this murder does not fit the severity of a penalty case. Luigi waited until Brian Thompson was walking alone and shot him with a gun. This is the most standard first degree murder you can get, with no extraordinary circumstances. Nobody was tortured, kidnapped, sexually assaulted, brutally beaten, etc… NOR did this crime involve children. When I think of a death penalty case, I think of crimes like these: • Chris Watts - murdered his pregnant wife and two children, and then disposed of the kids’ remains in oil tanks. • Karla Tucker - hacked her sleeping victims to death with a pickaxe • Michael Perry - he used a gun, but it was more than one person. He murdered an elderly woman for her red Camaro, and then her two grandsons because they needed their clicker to get back into the gated community after leaving to dispose of her body • John William King - a white supremecist that murdered a black man by tying him to the back of his pickup truck and dragging him. . Waiting for a guy outside of a hotel and shooting him twice is undeniably capital murder, but it lacks special circumstances. Seeking the death penalty for this case is absurd. The only reason that the death penalty is even being sought after is to try to discourage common folks from killing the 1%, despite the fact that the death penalty does not deter crime. Shooting a CEO is not on the same level of offense as dragging them down the road with a pickup truck, or hacking them to death with a pickaxe while they slept. I guess the DA finds shooting CEOs as unforgivably offensive.
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: Mama is so amazing
item.description: I am so grateful to everyone who kept up with everything tonight. All the babies are clean and mobile and nursing and fighting. This is best case scenario. I’m so excited to watch them grow up. And naming them is going to be so much fun!! Sleep well, Imgur
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: I think we’re done
item.description: Thank you SO much to everyone who followed along tonight. This was a very emotional night for this mama and me. She absolutely KILLED it. She was SO attentive to every single baby she birthed. They are all thriving thanks to this mama. I’m too young to be a grandma but I’ll accept it!
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: What Happened Today #64
item.description: Trump breaks things on purpose — and dares you to stop him. He tanks the markets, targets dissent, opens forests to industry, and turns the federal government into a personal vendetta machine — then smirks from the golf course and tells you to “get rich.” While he defunds disaster prep, mass-fires civil servants, and forces law firms to swear loyalty oaths, Obama and Harris are stepping up. So must we. Please be safe if you're rallying today. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist LINKS 1. https://apnews.com/article/donald-trump-tariffs-golf-813c7a300021445636f63fddbfd38c83 2. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/china-hits-back-trump-34-tariff-us-imports-rcna199665 3. https://www.yahoo.com/news/breaking-doj-seeks-87-month-013635741.html 4. https://www.militarytimes.com/pentagon/2025/04/04/hegseth-to-visit-panama-as-trump-says-us-is-reclaiming-its-canal/ 5. https://www.sfgate.com/national-parks/article/interior-secretary-national-parks-order-20259116.php 6. https://www.mediaite.com/news/trump-administration-mocks-judges-order-to-bring-back-maryland-father-from-salvadoran-prison-even-after-admitting-he-was-deported-in-error/ 7. https://www.cnbc.com/2025/04/04/trumps-extends-tiktok-second-time.html 8. https://www.msn.com/en-ca/entertainment/other/maya-angelou-memoir-holocaust-book-are-among-those-pulled-from-naval-academy-library-in-dei-purge/ar-AA1Ckqc2 9. https://apnews.com/article/irs-layoffs-civil-rights-compliance-trump-musk-d2c5708973cddf91c1a72b392118628c 10. https://www.fox5ny.com/news/columbia-student-records-congress-notice 11. https://abcnews.go.com/US/wireStory/trump-administration-ends-key-grant-program-helps-communities-120506359 12. https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2025/apr/04/supreme-court-teacher-grants-dei 13. https://thehill.com/policy/energy-environment/5233501-chemical-industry-pollution-exemptions-trump-biden/ 14. https://billingsgazette.com/article_e4f0667f-9c29-5e4d-9092-37de346a5172.html 15. https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2025/04/04/autism-vaccines-nih-research-trump/ 16. https://www.fox5dc.com/news/director-national-museum-african-american-history-culture-leave 17. https://www.wral.com/story/republican-jefferson-griffin-wins-nc-appeals-court-challenge-in-case-contesting-65k-ballots/21944370/ 18. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5233200-law-firms-judges-trump-executive-order-perkins-coie/ 19. https://www.cnn.com/2025/04/04/politics/obama-harris-rebuke-trump/index.html

Look at the lace over her toes!! I can’t wrap my head around how the artist made it look like it was transparent.
This lace. Wow.
The veil.
Switching gears.

item.title: She could do 60 days in jail
item.description: The did this on purpose but apparently not to be able to sue like some do, she was just mad about the situation. If she goes to jail, it will be a men's jail and she will have her hair forcefully cut and she will be denied hormones. Fuck Florida. https://apnews.com/article/florida-transgender-bathroom-arrest-be735cfe5eafa7f510692cfb3c58269e

item.title: All I want from Republicans is a “yup”

item.dc:creator: dirtyFrenchman

item.title: Yep
item.description: I have a confession to make: I was completely smitten by someone at the co-op near my house. I was so moved by their kindness that I can’t quite find the words to express it. Meeting them humbled me in a way I didn’t expect. They were so socially conscious, so considerate, and just full of good energy. I loved how they were so mindful of others. The people there were genuinely cool, with unique, fun styles—like the girl with the amazing hair and everyone with their cool piercings. But it wasn’t just their vibe that got me; they were part of something bigger. They ran this incredible food bank, providing free food for anyone in need. Nurses, retirees, students—everyone came, and it was just heartwarming. I didn’t need the food, but I was so grateful for what they were doing. Even though I’m overseas now, I think about them often and I hope they’re doing well. Thank you for being so amazing. Thank you to the guy at the co-op checkout, you know who you are. I still remember you, and you’re incredibly kind. I hope you’re doing alright. Thank you for showing me that neurodivergence is cool. Your understanding and kindness meant the world to me, and I’ll never forget you. Thank you.
item.dc:creator: Multipass

item.title: A different goodnight
item.description: I’m used to having to stop mama cat from following me out of the kitten room. I have a broken down box that I usually have to hold against my legs to keep her from leaving the room. But now she has her babies. And she doesn’t feel the need to run out. I’m going to have to get used to this
item.dc:creator: KittensForDays

item.title: Sign Language - 85 Vintage Images of Signs!
item.description:
Y'all gonna wait? 1975
Delphine, the waitress at La Grenouille, one of the oldest restaurants on the Left Bank, Paris, 1952 (photo by Bert Hardy)
Rick Astley's father Ossie celebrating Never Gonna Give You Up making it to #1 on the UK charts, 1987
New albums by Eddie Money, Cher and Judy Collins, 1979, Sunset Strip, CA (photo by Robert Landau)
Your surest weapon against impurity is frequent Holy Communion
After a snowstorm in Norwich, CT, 1940 (photo by Jack Delano)
Elvis Presley’s lavender Lincoln covered in love notes written in lipstick by fans, Miami, 1956
Free Advice, 1987 (photo by Joel Sternfeld)
Slave Dealer, Alexandria, VA, 1863
Donny Osmond fan, Manchester, England, 1973 (photo by Martin Parr)
Seen outside the Academy Awards, 1974
King Tut's Tomb, White City, Chicago, IL, 1923
Sign at entrance to Frank Sinatra’s Beverly Hills home, 1960s
Tower Records at Night, Sunset Strip, Hollywood, CA, 1977 (photo by Robert Landau)
I remember Crazy Eddie's commercials, Brooklyn NY, 1974
Underground Commonwealth Savings Bank and Post Office, Coober Pedy, South Australia, 1925
Bureau of Land Management
J. Baptiste hairdressing-barber's saloon, Hill End gold fields, NSW, Australia, 1872
Rice Bubbles! Sydney, Australia, 1930
Our true intent is all for your delight! Butlins Holiday Camp, England, 1982 (photo by Barry Lewis)
New Yorkers line up outside a meat market, 1946
Meard Street, Soho, London. This may not be vintage but I don't care.
Daytona Beach, FL with Clark Gable and a fish (photo by Berenice Abbott)
Segregation separation sign at bus station, Durham, NC, 1940 (photo by Jack Delano)
Sissy Spacek attends a screening of her film Carrie, 1976
Keep off my face, Pontchartrain Beach, New Orleans, 1963
Somewhere in the south, 1940s (photo by Alfred Eisenstaedt)
A ride on the Congo Belle at Weeki Wachi, FL, 1960s
Girl Scouts and Brownies selling cookies, 1962
The Blues explained with John Lee Hooker, 1951 (photo by Clemens Kalischer)
Camp Hale, Vail, CO, 1942 - Camp Vale was the training site of World War II American ski soldiers
Maps to Stars Homes and Pacino, Hollywood, CA, 1979 (photo by Robert Landau)
Pianist Fats Waller, his orchestra and The Creole Dancing Revue at Frank Sebastian's New Cotton Club, Culver City, CA, 1935
Beatniks at the Seven Arts Café, NYC, 1959 (photo by Burt Glinn)
Famous Malted Milk- Coca Cola Sign, Times Square, NYC, 1950 (photo by Ruth Orkin)
Bombers at the bar during WWII, Tampa, FL, 1942
Not just tamales....Brownsville, TX, 1939 (phto by Carl Mydans)
Tom Jones at Caesars Palace, Las Vegas, NV, 1982 (photo by Bertrand Laforêt)
Democratic Convention crowd tutside Americana Hotel, NYC, 1976 (photo by Ruth Orkin)
Supermarket, late 1960s
Medford, OR, newsstand, 1939 - How Do Radio Announcers Get That Way?
Cycle Sluts are in town! NYC, 1988 (photo by Matt Weber)
St. Mark's Place, East Village, NYC, 1969
Tilly overlooking the crowd at Steeplechase beach, Coney Island, NYC
Good luck to the Prospective Member! 1955
Continental Baths opening, NYC, 1968
Grapefruit display at the Imperial County Fair, CA, 1942
Highway approaching Hurst, IL, 1939 (photo by Arthur Rothstein)
Artwork by Larry Sultan, late 70s
Free hugs and kisses at the 1971 American Library Association Conference in Dallas, TX (photo by Grey Villet)
Weathermen with a Ouija board - God and Magic 8 Ball as a backup, 1965
Members of AIDS activist group ACT UP, Rockville, MD, 1988
Nags Head, NC, 1975
The Gap, Westwood Mall, Michigan, early 1980s
Healthy ice cream, 1930s
Personal message, NYC, 1973 (photo by Joel Sternfeld)
50 cent train rides, New Zealand, 1987 (photo by Kathryn McCool)
1968
Lust's Regent Theater, Wash DC, 1920
On Beale Street, Memphis, TN, 1939 (photo by Marion Post Wolcott)
Pink Floyd, The Wall billboard, 1979 (photo by Robert Landau)
Doc's Leisure Time, Philadelphia, 1981
Bat Cave
Stereo fair in front of Kmart, early 80s
Bad Hotel
Somewhere in India
Cour Saint-Guillaume, Paris, 1866
Los Angeles, CA, 1950s
Motorists in NJ got a preview of the odd/even numbered license plate gas rationing plan, 1973
I would actually prefer to see Bad News Bears, but this is tough choice! Albuquerque, NM, 1977 (photo by Robert Zuckerman)
Hotel Normandie, flophouse in NYC, 1980 (photo Jean Pierre Laffont)
Rent a Radio, NYC, 1953
1968
Market in Indianapolis Is it our way? Air way?
Holy Land USA, Waterbury, CT, 1979 (photo by Lisa Barlow)
Mitchell Building, State Library New South Wales, Australia, 1943 (photo by Ivan Ives) "In books lies the soul of the whole Past Time; the articulate audible voice of the Past when the body and the material substance of it has altogether vanished like a dream." Thomas Carlyle
Stevie Wonder, NYC, 1976
2000 is normally out of my range for "vintage", but this reserved sign is very funny, Los Angles (photo by Gary Leonard)
Highway signs at Trading Post, Datil, NM, 1940
Los Angeles, 1955
Muir Woods, CA, 1966
Guinness advertisement, London, 1949 (photo by Bert Hardy)
Hibachi House, Times Square, NYC, 1980s
Drive-In Fun, circa 1980 - I will always choose Meatballs!
Clara Bow in "It" - Royal Theater, Kansas City, MO, 1927
item.dc:creator: FAUST44
